inline_comments.message: Because linked list gives us better performance - insertion in arrays consume a lot of time.
use a constant, remove from method signature

no need to update the repository for the single method being invoked from test
findByDataspaceAndAnchorName(dataspace, anchorName).orElseThrow(); 
can be called from test directly
the repository change is out of scope for entire task, 
i suggest to rollback this change

non need for this, just provide the relationship
childDataNode.setParent(parentDataNode);
We cannot set parent, only set children.

this is incorrect. the anchorName will not be a part of Fragment
after Anchor decoupling, you need to set the AnchorEntity (currently Fragment)
object as .anchor(anchor) in order to populate the reference (anchor_id in database)
anchor object here, not a string

it seems reasonable to create both parent and child within this method, 
it's not justified creating parent in test, but child in method

this data is not expected in DataNode object when data is passed for persistence.
it also null here (in the test)

its an immutable collection, why to explicitly define it, if it should be replaced anyway.
keep consistency then - let it be not initialized by default as leaves map is

Niahm and I were actually talking about the use of immutable collections in tests. I though tit was OK not to as it is only a test. But maybe it is better to always use immutable collections and related utility classes to get used to them, even in tests
yes null might be better, we realized the lombok-generated builder wil use null anyway even if properties are set here...

as a reminder this needs to be replaced with getByDataspaceAndName() instead of findByDataspaceAndName().orElseThrow(); this will provide the validation as well.

it seems as dependency on https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/116629/6/cps-ri/src/main/java/org/onap/cps/spi/repository/FragmentRepository.java.

depends on which change to be merged first

i'd suggest creating separate data file i.e. fragments.sql
it seems reasonable to test cases with data being in db already

also it's necessary to test the error cases (dataspace or anchor missing, integrity violation as well), 
see no problem if these updates will be provided as additional change of same story

agree with both points
- need to split in separate test files (we can extend come base tets class if needed)
- additional test etc can be done in separate commit

I would like this test to have at least 3 generations to proof recursion is working
also reflect this in the names

testStoreDataNodeWithDecendants
or
testStoreDataNodeWithChidlrenAndGrandChildren
any specific reason for use LinkedList?

why this returns Fragment, return is never used
correct impl, will be to convert all fragments, then collect into list, and then process addChildFragments

Hi Claudio the return is used, see line 98. I worked a bit with Niamh on this but I do take your point maybe it is OK into do in 2 stages, first recurs to populate dataNode and all children
and then a separate method to populate the list for the DB...

-Im looking to obtain immutable list and then process. Other type of approach all the times ends on some race condition or issue when passing a list and modifying it.
- Sorry, I missed the line where it is used. If it can be reworked as per my intentions, great. I will take another look later, maybe I can provide another input
Because linked list gives us better performance when inserting data
I wonder WHY to do store parent instead of children in FragmentEntity, can this be changed, which is better for later use case like adding/removing child fragments?!

we tried, see patch #10. The problem is for some reason the Fragment Entity stores its parent and not its children (unlike the dataNode object). the only way to split the code into 2 separate iterations (which I do prefer) is to add a a transient collection in fragment. Not sure if I like that...

we need to make a choice which approach we prefer this oen in patch #9 recursing and populating in one method (we can come up with better names) or the approach in patch #10 with the additional transient field... 

In any case i do wonder WHY to do store parent instead of children in FragmentEntity, can this be changed, which is better for later use case like adding/removing child fragments?!
I'll ask the same question in patch 10 in case we do want to continue with that alternative
it as bigger memory consumption, so for big volumes of data it will have higher impact. Up to the team if they have some comments on this

why are you using findAll? why find by key (hardcode) 
finding by xPath will be another user story anyway
see comment below on extracting out common method
3 similar blocks -> extract out assertFragment method
i would probably swap these 2 lines :-)
should be handled in the AnchorRepository
I agree this can be extracted to a common method

Question:
As this is a OneToMany relation, meaning one parent could have multiple child.
Is it possible that initially one child is added and other children are added later?

If this is possible, then I wonder if this could lead to parent also being inserted everytime a new request for storing child for the parent is requested thus leading to constraint violation error.
why not use CpsAdminPersistenceService? implementation for this service duplicate codes already present on CpsAdminPersistenceService implementation
ignore comment about duplication, regarding CpsAdminPersistenceService I still think that we could keep everything in only 2 types of persistence service (Admin / Module)

Hi Claudio, we are currently following the SPI proposal. 
https://wiki.onap.org/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=92999716

Hi Aditya, good question but I'm not sure how to answer this right now.
We currently have a story on the backlog for adding another child to a datanode that has a child. I think this will be tested/investigated as part of that story https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-164
